title: linum viscosum var. pyrenaicum
intro: Linum viscosum var. pyrenaicum, also known as the sticky flax, is a plant species in the flax family (Linaceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula and the Pyrenees. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to have potential health benefits in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Linum viscosum var. pyrenaicum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. Linum viscosum var. pyrenaicum can be prepared as an infusion or tincture for medicinal use, and its seeds and leaves can be used in herbal preparations.
